text.title: LLM Drift Daily Triage Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for a daily 15-minute LLM drift triage ritual.

rule.statement: Triage takes ≤15 minutes/day. Beyond that the runner template is incomplete and humans paper over missing tooling.
rule.rationale: 15 minutes is the rough budget for a sustainable daily ritual; over budget the ritual is dropped first when sprint pressure spikes.

rule.statement: Each day's report carries three deltas — eval score, refusal-rate, cost-per-call — plus three failing-trace summaries.
rule.rationale: Three is enough to spot a regression and not so many the human eye glazes; trace summaries anchor metric numbers to concrete cases.

rule.statement: A named on-call owns the day's triage. Default-assignment fallback exists when on-call has no calendar slot.
rule.rationale: Unowned ritual rots; default fallback keeps the chain unbroken on holidays.

rule.statement: Pre-defined escalation thresholds: eval drop >2pp → mitigate, >5pp → escalate (page); refusal-rate drift >3pp → mitigate, >8pp → escalate; cost >10% → mitigate, >25% → escalate.
rule.rationale: Threshold-driven escalation removes "is this serious?" debate at the moment of triage.

rule.statement: Weekly trend review (Mondays) looks at 7 daily deltas; persistent <threshold drift (4+ consecutive days same direction) escalates regardless of single-day magnitude.
rule.rationale: Slow drift never trips daily thresholds but compounds; the weekly check catches it.

rule.statement: The daily report is append-only — no retro edits. Corrections go in a follow-up entry referencing the original.
rule.rationale: Retro edits destroy the audit trail; append-only preserves the actual decision history.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → run the daily procedure and produce triage-report.json passing the validator.
No production traffic or no eval set → skip; bootstrap the eval first.
